title: Dal Makhani

overview.description: A rich and creamy North Indian dal made with whole black urad dal and kidney beans, slowly cooked with tomatoes, butter and aromatic spices until silky smooth.

method: Soak the urad dal and kidney beans overnight in plenty of cold water.
Drain and rinse the beans, then cover with fresh water and simmer until completely tender, around 1½–2 hours.
Heat the butter and vegetable oil in a large saucepan over a medium heat.
Cook the onions for 10–12 minutes until soft and lightly browned.
Add the garlic, ginger and green chilli and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, turmeric and Kashmiri chilli powder and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the chopped tomatoes and cook until thickened, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the cooked dal and kidney beans along with enough of their cooking liquid to create a thick but spoonable consistency.
Simmer gently for 45–60 minutes, stirring regularly to prevent sticking.
Lightly mash some of the beans against the side of the pan to create a naturally creamy texture.
Stir in the garam masala, double cream and season with salt.
Finish with fresh coriander before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Dal Makhani originated in Punjab and literally means 'buttery lentils'. Traditionally it is cooked slowly for many hours to develop its famous creamy texture.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How slow cooking develops flavour, how to prepare dried pulses and how to create creaminess without blending.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the richest vegetarian dishes in the planner and an excellent showcase for pantry ingredients. Perfect for freezing and one of the family's regular Indian meals.
chapman-tips: Don't rush the cooking—time is the secret ingredient.
Mash some of the lentils rather than blending the curry.
Serve with homemade naan for a restaurant-quality meal.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in meal-sized portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Often even better the following day after the flavours have developed further.
